name: La Defensa del Sur Route
name_original: Desde Banyeres de Mariola hasta Biar – Circular por el Camino del Cid
description: This moderate 20.9-mile touring cycling route through Parc Natural de la Serra de Mariola offers castles, river gorges, and diverse terrain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Banyeres De Mariola offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, characterized by rugged landscapes and elevations up to 818 meters in the town center. The region features pleasant forests of stone pine, kermes oaks, and ash trees, alongside dramatic rock formations and river gorges. The Vinalopó River crosses the municipal district, providing routes that explore its headwaters and historical water mills. Numerous mountain peaks and natural springs contribute to the varied topography, making it suitable for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Banyeres De Mariola The most popular touring cycling route is La Defensa del Sur Route, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route features moderate climbs and varied landscapes within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plaza de la Constitución, Biar – Biar loop from Banyeres de Mariola, a difficult 28.6 miles (46.0 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and historical sites. Local touring cyclists also love the Cava Gran de Agres – Cova de l'Habitació loop from Banyeres de Mariola, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Banyeres De Mariola is defined by the varied terrain of the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, the Vinalopó River, and numerous mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Banyeres De Mariola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Banyeres De Mariola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on touring cycling routes around Banyeres de Mariola?
A: The touring cycling routes in Banyeres de Mariola, particularly within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, offer a diverse experience. You'll encounter rugged terrain with significant elevations, including peaks over a thousand meters, as well as pleasant forests and river gorges. The routes range from moderate, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952699251/la-defensa-del-sur-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Defensa del Sur Route&lt;/a&gt; with its varied landscapes, to more difficult paths such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17307246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Constitución, Biar – Biar loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes in the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park involve challenging terrain, sections of the Vía Verde del Xixarra offer an easier, well-signposted experience suitable for families. This charming cycle path follows an old railway line, providing scenic beauty through vineyards and dry valleys, and is generally accessible for various users, including those with wheelchairs. It's a great option for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: What natural features and historical sites can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. You can explore the dramatic rock formations and diverse flora of the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, including the headwaters of the Vinalopó River. Historical sites include the Banyeres Castle, which offers expansive views, and the ancient water mills along the 'Route of the Molins'. You might also encounter natural springs like the Font de la Coveta. For specific natural monuments, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3116128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mariola Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area around Banyeres de Mariola features several beautiful waterfalls. You might find routes that pass near or offer views of cascades like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4412394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Polop Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4611533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantanet Ravine Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4412520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salt Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These natural attractions provide refreshing stops and scenic points of interest during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Banyeres de Mariola?
A: Banyeres de Mariola offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, but the higher parts of the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park offer cooler air. Winter can be chilly, but often still suitable for cycling on clear days.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Banyeres de Mariola are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres – Cova de l'Habitació loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32031629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Barbara Hermitage – Mariola Spring loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that take you through diverse landscapes within the natural park.

Q: Can I reach Banyeres de Mariola by public transport with my bike?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, regional buses in Spain sometimes allow bikes, often requiring them to be disassembled or placed in a bag. It's advisable to check with the specific bus operator for routes connecting to Banyeres de Mariola regarding their bike policies and any potential restrictions or fees before planning your journey.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing touring cycling trails?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Banyeres de Mariola, especially near the town center or at designated access points for the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. Many routes start from the town itself, making it convenient to park in municipal areas. For specific trailheads, look for local signage indicating parking facilities.

Q: Do I need any special permits to cycle in the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for touring cycling on established routes within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of environmental protection. For any specific or unusual activities, it's best to consult the official park authority's guidelines.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Banyeres de Mariola?
A: The touring cycling experience in Banyeres de Mariola is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views from the mountain peaks, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. The mix of challenging ascents and pleasant forest paths is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling connections or routes that pass through Banyeres de Mariola?
A: Yes, Banyeres de Mariola is part of broader cycling networks. Sections of the historic Camino del Cid pass through the area, offering a cultural and scenic journey. Additionally, the region is featured in collections like the 'Iron Gravel 360' and 'Discover Alicante – Costa Blanca Inland Path (GR 330)'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38195869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section along the Camino del Cid – Canyon and Bridge near Villena loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that connects to these longer adventures.

Q: Can I rent a touring bicycle in or near Banyeres de Mariola?
A: While specific touring bike rental shops directly in Banyeres de Mariola might be limited, it's often possible to find rental services in larger nearby towns or cities in the Alicante province. It's recommended to search online or contact local tourist information centers in advance to arrange for bike rental, especially if you require a specific type of touring bicycle.
tips: Very beautiful old town, over which the castle towers majestically. Worth a detour! The name of the city is of Arabic origin and was first mentioned at the end of the 11th century.

The historical center of Biar bears witness to a rich medieval history. Like so many Spanish villages, it consists of a labyrinth of narrow and steep streets where it is easy to get lost. The Castillo on the mountain top is the town's landmark and can be seen from afar. The Castilian knight and Spanish national hero El Cid is also said to have passed this way.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the La Defensa del Sur Route?
faqs.answer: The specific circular route from Banyeres de Mariola to Biar, as detailed on komoot, typically takes around 2 hours and 5 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. However, the broader La Defensa del Sur Route, which is part of the Way of El Cid and spans 250-264 kilometers, is generally recommended to be completed over 3-4 days.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of terrains. You'll find well-developed farm tracks and 'vías verdes' (reconditioned railway tracks), alongside quiet tarmac roads. Over 90% of the route is on paved surfaces. While generally low to medium difficulty, there are some challenging mountainous sections, particularly between Ontinyent and Bocairent, where you might need to dismount and push your bike. For gravel bikes, it's considered 100% cyclable on rural roads with good surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the La Defensa del Sur Route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered to have a low to medium difficulty, making it accessible for many cyclists. While there are some challenging ascents, especially in mountainous areas, the majority of the route is on well-surfaced roads. If you're comfortable with moderate climbs and have a touring or gravel bike, you should find it enjoyable. Beginners might consider the shorter, circular section from Banyeres de Mariola to Biar as a good introduction.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The La Defensa del Sur Route is ideal for cycling during autumn, winter, or spring. These seasons offer more pleasant temperatures for exploring the diverse landscapes and historical sites without the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the route?
faqs.answer: For the circular route, you can typically find parking in the towns of Banyeres de Mariola or Biar, which serve as excellent starting and ending points. These towns are well-connected and offer amenities for cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the route or the Parc Natural de la Serra de Mariola?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the La Defensa del Sur Route itself. As the trail passes through the Parc Natural de la Serra de Mariola, standard park regulations apply, but typically there are no fees for general access. Always check local guidelines for any specific activities or protected areas within the park.

faqs.question: What can I see along the La Defensa del Sur Route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in diverse scenery and historical sites. You'll encounter impressive landscapes ranging from the coastal lagoon of La Albufera Natural Park and the extensive orchards of Huerta de Valencia, to picturesque river gorges and spectacular cliffs near Bocairent. Historically, you'll pass through medieval towns like Alzira, Xàtiva, and Bocairent, and see numerous castles that formed part of the Almohad defensive line, linking towns such as Banyeres de Mariola, Biar, Villena, Sax, and Petrel.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Defensa del Sur Route is part of the larger Way of El Cid. It also passes through sections of other official trails, including El Camí IT5TR4 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, and various segments of the Via Verda del Xitxarra.

faqs.question: Is the La Defensa del Sur Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows roads and tracks, specific regulations for dogs can vary, especially when passing through towns or protected natural areas like the Parc Natural de la Serra de Mariola. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What makes this route historically significant?
faqs.answer: The La Defensa del Sur Route is notable for its deep historical and literary connection to the Way of El Cid. It traces the lands and towns that Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar, El Cid, sought to defend after his conquest of Valencia. This section focuses on the inland defense line and the castles built to protect against Almohad attacks, offering a unique journey through medieval history.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or hidden gems along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the well-known castles and medieval towns, the route offers magnificent views from challenging ascents, particularly in the mountainous areas between Ontinyent and Bocairent. The Palmeral of Elche, a UNESCO World Heritage Site with ancient palm groves, is a unique natural highlight as the route descends towards the Bajo Vinalopó. Keep an eye out for the diverse animal and plant species in La Albufera Natural Park, known for its beautiful reflections.
